claude_cli.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools Read,Grep,Glob --disallowedTools Bash,Edit,Write,MultiEdit,NotebookEdit --output-format json

findings.title: S11 incorrectly closes M03 and skips S12-S20
findings.summary: S11 no longer sets completesSourceMicroPhase/source_micro_phase_completed true and now hands off to RP00.P02.M03.S12 across code, contract, fixture, tests, README, and validator.

findings.title: Add ledger successor cross-check for terminal/non-terminal closeout state
findings.summary: The RP00 contract validator now derives the S11 terminal flag and successor from docs/weighted-implementation-ledger.json and fails if S11 is treated as terminal.

findings.title: Tests locked in the bad S11 boundary
findings.summary: Tests now assert non-terminal S11 behavior and RP00.P02.M03.S12 handoff.

required_followups: Continue with RP00.P02.M03.S12 Persistence boundary as the next subphase.
review_notes: Claude confirmed the metadata-only lock-rule logic, validators, fixture parity, and fail-closed claim handling were otherwise correctly implemented.
The blocking defect was a false source microphase closeout boundary, not runtime lock behavior.
Adjudication fixed the false boundary and added ledger-derived guard coverage so the same class of error fails validation.
parsed_review.summary: Claude reported FAIL / NO_GO because S11 falsely closed RP00.P02.M03 and routed to RP00.P02.M04.S01. The P0 boundary defect, P2 ledger guard recommendation, and P3 test drift have been fixed; after adjudication S11 is PASS_WITH_FINDINGS / GO with no unresolved P0/P1/P2.

raw_claude_cli_result_summary.summary: Claude returned FAIL / NO_GO with one P0 false closeout boundary finding, one P2 ledger guard recommendation, and test drift noted as part of the P0 remediation.
